name: Cdkn2a-targeted OSK extended lifespan and health markers in mouse aging models
summary: Sahu 2024 adds a targeted partial-reprogramming animal signal using aged- or stressed-cell promoter control, with lifespan, inflammatory, hematopoietic, wound-healing, and tumor-incidence boundaries.
statement: AAV Cdkn2a-OSK expression in aged or stressed cells reduced inflammatory cytokine expression and extended lifespan in progeroid mice, delayed aging phenotypes and extended lifespan in naturally aged wild-type mice without altered tumor incidence in the abstract, and improved wound healing in aged mice.
population_or_model: HGPS mouse model, naturally aged wild-type mice, and aging or stressed human primary fibroblasts
time_horizon: Single-dose AAV Cdkn2a-OSK treatment with lifespan, tissue, wound-healing, and safety follow-up in mouse models
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports lifespan extension but does not provide a single effect size.
caveats: This remains animal and human-cell evidence, not human outcomes evidence.
Targeted promoter control may reduce but does not settle delivery, off-target, dose, immune-response, or long-term safety risk.
The no-altered-tumor-incidence statement is an abstract-level safety signal that requires source-fidelity review.
